previous_statement: (Definition of face dimension spectrum and gap). For a unital *-algebra A, define the face dimension spectrum Spec(A) = {dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) : φ ∈ S(A)} ⊆ N_0 ∪ {∞}. Note 0 ∈ Spec(A) always (pure states exist by Krein-Milman). The face dimension gap G(A) = {d ∈ N_{>0} : d ∉ Spec(A)} is the set of positive integers that do not appear as face dimensions. Equivalently, by the face-commutant correspondence, Spec(A) = {dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1 : φ ∈ S(A)}, so d ∈ Spec(A) iff there exists a representation of A whose commutant has self-adjoint real dimension d + 1.
new_statement: (Definition of face dimension spectrum and gap). Let A be a unital C*-algebra (or more generally an Archimedean order unit space), so that the state space S(A) is weak-* compact. Define the face dimension spectrum Spec(A) = {dim(F_{S(A)}(phi)) : phi in S(A)} subset of N_0 union {infty}, where dim denotes the affine dimension of the face (equivalently, topological dimension; these agree for faces of compact convex sets, and both give infinity for infinite-dimensional faces). Note 0 in Spec(A) always: pure states exist by the Krein-Milman theorem (applicable since S(A) is compact convex). The face dimension gap is G(A) = {d in N_{>0} : d not in Spec(A)}. Equivalently, by the face-commutant correspondence (node 1.1), Spec(A) = {dim_R(pi_phi(A)'^{sa}) - 1 : phi in S(A)} where pi_phi is the GNS (cyclic) representation associated to phi. Thus d in Spec(A) iff there exists a GNS representation of A whose commutant has self-adjoint real dimension d + 1.
